texts.full_text: for turning back kick, a kick employed in Karate. The kick is a basic move but theexecution of it can be varied to suit different situations. The Ushiro-Geri (Turning Back Kick) has many variations and applications depending on how you execute this technique: 1.) As an offensive weapon 2.) To counterattack or block incoming attacks from your opponent, especially when he/she is in close range. This kick will also help create some distance between the two of you. 3.) When used as a defensive move to protect yourself against any attack coming at you while moving backwards (i.e., Ushiro-Geri followed by Mae-geri). 4.) As an offensive weapon, this technique can be executed from different angles and positions such that it will become difficult for your opponent(s)to anticipate which direction the kick is going. 5.) The turning backkick may also serve as a distraction or feint to lure in opponents into attacking you so they leave themselves open when trying unsuccessfully at hitting their target with an overhand punch, etc., thus allowing them time enough before being able react accordingly by executing another technique like Ushiro-geri again but this one aimed towards the head area instead of just below it as was done previously. 6.) As a defensive move against incoming attacks from opponents who are in close range and trying to hit you with an overhand punch or other similar type attack, etc., thus allowing them time enough before being able react accordingly by executing another technique like Ushiro-geri again but this one aimed towards the head area instead of just below it as was done previously. 7.) As a defensive move against incoming attacks from opponents who are in close range and trying to hit you with an overhand punch or othe

texts.full_text: movies like Kunwara Baap . Plot Polly Parrish (Ginger Rogers) is a salesgirl at the department store John B. Merlin and Son in New York City. Hiredfor her experience being six months pregnant with children who need school supplies, she incurs complaints from co-workers upon leaving for lunch in full view of everyone else taking advantage of office conditions unseen
